chief_complaint: Transient neurological deficit with melena in elderly hypertensive patient
clinical_reasoning: Elderly hypertensive male with transient neurological symptoms (visual disturbance, confusion — resolved) and melena. The combination is unusual and warrants urgent evaluation. While two separate pathologies (TIA + GI bleed) are possible, the simultaneous presentation in a patient with history of bicuspid aortic valve and aortic dilation raises concern for aortic pathology as a unifying diagnosis. Aortic dissection can cause malperfusion of multiple vascular territories simultaneously. Bilateral BP measurement is recommended as an immediate screening step. ORANGE — the diagnostic uncertainty and potential for rapidly deteriorating aortic pathology warrant urgent physician assessment.
vital_sign_concerns: BP 152/88 — hypertensive, measured in one arm only
risk_factors: Bicuspid aortic valve with known aortic dilation
